user_query:
Act as a supervisor gate for a main agent that tends to ask me obvious permission-loop questions. Apply a simple policy: AUTO for safe, reversible internal actions with clear defaults; CONFIRM for external send/publish, destructive/irreversible actions, money/account changes, or production/legal/compliance risk; ESCALATE only if blocked after reasonable retries or when there are multiple materially different paths.

Do the following using the provided inputs and write all outputs under output/:
1) Read input/drafts.jsonl (each line has: id, task_context, draft, and may include simple flags). For each draft:
   - Classify decision_class as AUTO, CONFIRM, or ESCALATE.
   - Set triage_class to one of: FINE, NEEDS_NUDGE, STUCK, DONE, ESCALATE.
   - Set worth_trying (true/false) using a low-cost, reversible, internal heuristic.
   - Provide assumptions ([]) and defaults_used ([]) when you choose AUTO or when helpful.
   - Provide recommended_next_action (string) for every item.
   - Provide one_question_if_needed (string or null). Only include a compact one-question escalation when decision_class is CONFIRM or ESCALATE.
   - Provide reasoning_summary (<= 220 characters) that explains the choice concisely.
   - Set task_state_path to output/.tasks/supervisor-demo.md for all items.
   Write one JSON object per line to output/decisions.jsonl in the same order as input.

2) Create output/rewrite.md that contains, in order, one section per input id with this exact section format:
   - A heading line: "## <id>"
   - A line: "Decision: <AUTO|CONFIRM|ESCALATE>"
   - A line: "Message: <the user-visible rewritten message>"
   Rewriting rules:
   - For AUTO: do not ask me a question; state the chosen default(s) and proceed. Suppress obvious permission loops.
   - For CONFIRM: ask exactly one compact approval question about the specific external/destructive action; keep it brief.
   - For ESCALATE: state situation and what you tried, propose a recommended default, and ask exactly one compact question to unblock.

3) Read input/large_task_brief.md and create a lightweight checkpoint file at output/.tasks/supervisor-demo.md with these exact labeled fields (one per line, in this order):
   - Title:
   - Status: (use one of: active, paused, done, stuck, escalate)
   - Started:
   - Last Updated:
   - Completed Steps:
     - (at least two bullet points summarizing concrete completed steps)
   - Current Blocker:
   - Next Step:
   Keep this concise and actionable so I can skim it instead of getting interrupting check-in messages.

General expectations:
- Default to execution for low-risk internal work and pick reasonable defaults without asking me.
- Only ask me to confirm when it’s truly required or escalate when genuinely blocked.
- Keep reasoning_summary short, and make the rewritten messages crisp and user-ready.
